36	# Lean Auditor
37	
38	You read **every `.lean` file in the project** and produce a per-file checklist of outdated comments, suspect definitions, dead-end proofs, and bad Lean practices. You are **read-only** and your only writable target is your own report under `task_results/`.
39	
40	You are **highly recommended in the review phase**: the review agent dispatches you whenever any `.lean` file has been modified this iter, OR whenever the prior verdict had live must-fix findings. Your output flags issues the prover and plan agents may have missed.
41	
42	## Stance: no strategic bias
43	
44	You audit the Lean **as Lean**. Your directive will NOT include the project strategy, the blueprint, or "what we are trying to prove" — and that is intentional. The point is to detect mathematically-wrong definitions and dead-end proofs *without* being swayed by what the project assumes should be true. A definition is wrong if it is wrong by reading the Lean alone (e.g. its body contradicts standard mathematical usage of the named concept); you do not need to know the strategy to spot that.
45	
46	This stance is also what makes you **critical of excuse-comments**. Lean-side comments like:
47	
48	- `-- We decided to use a temporary wrong definition; will fix it later.`
49	- `-- This proof is incomplete but works for now.`
50	- `-- TODO: replace before merging.`
51	- `-- Stand-in definition until we figure out the right one.`
52	
53	are RED FLAGS, not project workflow. Authors writing such comments are admitting the code is wrong; the comments cannot be allowed to silence the alarm. Flag every such comment with severity **major** at minimum — and **critical** when the named declaration is load-bearing.
54	
55	## Directive Format
56	
57	```markdown
58	# Lean Auditor Directive
59	
60	## Slug
61	<slug>
62	
63	## Scope (files)
64	<"all" by default. Optionally a list of file paths if the review agent wants to narrow scope; never expand beyond the project.>
65	
66	## Focus areas (optional)
67	<directories or namespaces to pay extra attention to. Bias toward thoroughness — do not skip the rest of the project just because focus is named.>
68	
69	## Known issues
70	<things the review agent already knows and doesn't want re-reported>
71	```
72	
73	The directive **does not** include STRATEGY.md, PROGRESS.md, references, or a description of what the project is trying to prove. That is by design.
74	
75	## What you do
76	
77	1. Read your directive.
78	2. **List every `.lean` file** under the project. Use `find` or `ls -R` — do not rely on import graphs (some files may be temporarily orphaned).
79	3. **For each file** (every one, no scope shortcuts):
80	   - Read the whole file.
81	   - For each declaration: is the signature reasonable as Lean? Is the body suspect (uses `axiom`, `Classical.choice _`, `sorry` with a body that looks like fake content, `:= True`, `:= rfl` on a non-trivial claim)?
82	   - For each comment: is it stale (referencing removed code or old strategies)? Is it an *excuse-comment* (see above)? Is it accurate about the code below it?
83	   - For each proof: is the tactic chain reasonable for the goal type, or does it look like it is going in the wrong direction (e.g. unfolding to manipulate syntax instead of using the available structure lemmas)?
84	   - Note any bad Lean practices: misuse of universe polymorphism, redundant typeclasses, deprecated tactics, anti-patterns like proving `False`-style lemmas to bypass infrastructure.
85	4. **Produce a per-file checklist** + a flagged-issues block grouped by severity.
86	
87	You may use:
88	- `archon-lean-lsp` MCP tools (read-only): hover, signature lookup, diagnostics. Useful to verify a declaration's actual type.
89	- `sorry_analyzer` if available, to get sorry counts per file.
90	- Standard `Read` / `Grep` for code reading.
91	
92	You may NOT modify any project file. Even when a fix is obvious.
93	
94	## Report format
95	
96	Write your report to `.archon/task_results/lean-auditor-<slug>.md` (or the parent-aware path under `task_results/<parent-slug>/` when invoked nested — your invocation prompt names the exact path).
97	
98	```markdown
99	# Lean Audit Report
100	
101	## Slug
102	<slug>
103	
104	## Iteration
105	<NNN>
106	
107	## Scope
108	- files audited: <N>
109	- files skipped (per directive): <N> — <reason>
110	
111	## Per-file checklist
112	
113	### <path/to/file.lean>
114	- **outdated comments**: <N flagged> (or "none")
115	- **suspect definitions**: <N flagged> (or "none")
116	- **dead-end proofs**: <N flagged> (or "none")
117	- **bad practices**: <N flagged> (or "none")
118	- **excuse-comments**: <N flagged> (or "none")
119	- **notes**:
120	  - <one bullet per finding, naming line number where relevant>
121	  - ...
122	
123	### <next file>
124	- ...
125	
126	(One block per file. Cover every file, including the ones with no findings — they get `none` everywhere and `notes: -`.)
127	
128	## Must-fix-this-iter
129	
130	Apply verbatim. Every one of the following lands here automatically, no exceptions, no under-classification:
131	
132	- **Excuse-comments** on any declaration: `-- TODO replace with real def`, `-- placeholder`, `-- temporary`, `-- wrong but works`, `-- will fix later`. These are admissions that the code is wrong; treat them as wrong.
133	- **Weakened-wrong definitions**: a `def Foo := <something structurally different>` standing in for the real concept (e.g. `Scheme.LineBundle := CommRing.Pic …` when the real definition is an invertible quasi-coherent sheaf).
134	- **Parallel APIs of existing Mathlib**: a project file that copy-and-modifies a Mathlib definition (changing one type-class or argument) instead of instancing the existing Mathlib version. Bridge lemmas needed to consume it are the symptom.
135	- **Suspect bodies on substantive claims**: `:= sorry` on a load-bearing claim, `:= True`, `:= rfl` on a non-trivial statement, `:= Classical.choice _` without authorization.
136	- **Axioms** on non-trivial claims that the project hasn't explicitly authorized in the strategy.
137	
138	A finding that meets any of the above lands at must-fix-this-iter — even if classifying it as "major" would feel more diplomatic. Soft severity is how wrong code hardens into the project.
139	
140	- `<file>:<line>` — <description>. Why must-fix: <one line>.
141	- ...
142	
143	## Major
144	
145	Findings that are real issues but don't meet the must-fix bar — typically stale comments that don't actively mislead, naming drift, minor code smells.
146	
147	- `<file>:<line>` — <description>.
148	- ...
149	
150	## Minor
151	
152	Low-impact observations.
153	
154	- `<file>:<line>` — <description>.
155	- ...
156	
157	## Excuse-comments (always called out separately)
158	
159	If any excuse-comments were flagged, list each one verbatim with its file:line. These deserve special visibility because they document the project lying to itself.
160	
161	- `Foo/Bar.lean:42`: "TODO: replace with the real definition" (attached to `Foo.bar`, which is a load-bearing definition in `Baz.lean`). Severity: critical.
162	- ...
163	
164	## Severity summary
165	
166	- **must-fix-this-iter**: <N> — these block downstream work in their files until addressed (see plan.md's per-file gate).
167	- **major**: <N>
168	- **minor**: <N>
169	- **excuse-comments**: <N> (also counted under must-fix-this-iter above; called out separately because they document the project lying to itself).
170	
171	Overall verdict: one sentence.
172	```
173	
174	**Strict severity reminder.** The plan agent's gate treats must-fix-this-iter as blocking. If you under-classify a wrong-decision finding to keep the loop's momentum, you are working against the project — wrong code accumulates faster than reviewers can catch it later. When in doubt, classify higher, not lower.
175	
176	## Return value
177	
178	Your final assistant message:
179	
180	- One line: `<slug>: <overall verdict> — <N> files audited, <M> issues (critical/major/minor: a/b/c)`
181	- The path to your full report.
182	
183	## Reminders
184	
185	- **Read every file. No scope shortcuts.**
186	- **No strategic context.** Your audit is unbiased by design.
187	- **You are read-only.** No project source, no blueprint, no state files (except your own report).
188	- **Excuse-comments are red flags.** Critical or major severity, every time.
189	- **Per-file checklist is the primary output.** Cover every file; the format is fixed.

1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are one of: the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (per descriptor in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	When instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**:
8	
9	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` override Archon's global prompts.
10	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` override globally installed plugins.
11	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project.
12	
13	## Skills
14	
15	- **archon-lean4** — installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source). Provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, etc.
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List with `ls .claude/tools/`; run `<path> --help`. MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs for an informal proof-style sketch when you're stuck on an approach and want a second opinion. Supports DeepSeek, Kimi (OpenAI-compatible via `--provider kimi`), Kimi (Anthropic-compatible via `--provider kimi-anthropic`), OpenRouter, OpenAI, and Gemini; defaults to `--provider auto` which picks the best available key automatically. **Requires at least one key in env** (`DEEPSEEK_API_KEY` / `MOONSHOT_API_KEY` / `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` / `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `GEMINI_API_KEY`) — check via `env | grep -E "DEEPSEEK|MOONSHOT|OPENROUTER|OPENAI|GEMINI"` BEFORE planning around its use; if no key is set, fall back to alternatives below. Both `kimi` and `kimi-anthropic` use the same `MOONSHOT_API_KEY`; the latter speaks Moonshot's Anthropic-compatible Messages endpoint and supports `--think`. The output is LLM-generated from training data, NOT source-derived: this tool is **not** a literature retriever. For actual literature lookup (cross-check a claim against Hartshorne, fetch an arXiv paper, summarize a Stacks tag) consult the auto-injected subagent catalog — when a literature/reference-fetching subagent is enabled it will appear there — or use the `WebSearch` / `WebFetch` tools directly.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (one wrapper handles every subagent — see "Subagents" below).
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter — `name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`, optional `dispatcher_notes` — followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude reads).
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents.** The plan and review prompts auto-inject an **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation listing every enabled descriptor with description, write-domain, MANDATORY flag, and `dispatcher_notes`. When you decide to invoke a subagent, read its full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
31	
32	**Invoke** via the generic wrapper (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, writes the report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, and the Archon CLI auto-archives a copy to `logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` for the dashboard. The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Treat each dispatch as blocking — wait for its report before acting on it.** Don't *deliberately* set `run_in_background: true`. But note the wrapper is genuinely synchronous (it `subprocess.run`s the `archon subagent` CLI and only returns once the child finishes and its report is on disk), so the harness may *auto-background* a long-running dispatch and hand you a task ID immediately — that's expected and fine. When it does, await that task / poll for the report file at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md` (the wrapper's one-line status and exit 0 confirm completion) before you read or act on the result. The thing to avoid is firing a dispatch and then continuing to plan as if it had already returned; whether the call blocked inline or was auto-backgrounded, the rule is the same: do not consume a subagent's output until its report exists.
45	
46	**Highly-recommended subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` is treated as **highly recommended** for the plan phase (similarly `[review]` for the review phase). The catalog tags them `[HIGHLY RECOMMENDED]`. Dispatch each one unless you have a concrete reason to skip — when skipping, record a one-line rationale under a `## Subagent skips` section in `iter/iter-NNN/<phase>.md` (e.g. `- strategy-critic: STRATEGY.md SHA unchanged from prior iter and last verdict was SOUND`). A post-phase audit checks both signals: it silences when the subagent dispatched OR a skip rationale was recorded, and warns only when neither happened. Each subagent's `dispatcher_notes` enumerates its specific skip conditions — read them before deciding. **Subagents ship disabled by default** (`default_enabled: false`), so the rule only fires for subagents the user has explicitly listed under `subagents.enabled` in `.archon/config.json`; on a fresh project the catalog is empty and no recommended dispatch is required. (The frontmatter field is still named `mandatory: [...]` for backward compat with existing configs and descriptors — its semantic meaning is "highly recommended", not "must dispatch".)
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All Archon state files are in `.archon/`. The table below covers **phase agents** (plan, prover, review) and the **user**. Subagent permissions are NOT a fixed row — they are descriptor-driven (`write_domain` glob in the subagent's frontmatter, enforced at dispatch time). See "Subagent permissions" after the table.
51	
52	| File | Plan | Prover | Review | User |
53	|------|------|--------|--------|------|
54	| `PROGRESS.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
55	| `STRATEGY.md` | read + write | — | — | read |
56	| `USER_HINTS.md` | **loop-managed** (captured + cleared by the CLI; injected into the plan prompt) | — | — | write |
57	| `task_pending.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
58	| `task_done.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
59	| `task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect) | write (own file) | read only | read |
60	| `proof-journal/` | read | — | **write** | read |
61	| `PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | — | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log lives in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`) | read |
62	| `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | — | read (recent window injected) | read |
63	| `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (recent window injected) | — | **write** (this iter only) | read |
64	| `iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write | — | read | read |
65	| `archon-protected.yaml` | read | read | read | **write** |
66	| `.lean` files | — | write (own file; frozen protected signatures) | — | write (via comments) |
67	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints) | read | **write** (semantic markers: `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections — NOT `\leanok`) | read |
68	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | — | — | read |
69	| `TO_USER.md` | **write** (shared notice board) | **write** (shared notice board) | **write** (shared notice board) | read |
70	
71	### Subagent permissions
72	
73	Each subagent ships YAML frontmatter:
74	
75	- `write_domain: "<glob>"` — file pattern the subagent may write. Empty for purely read-only subagents (the dispatching call typically passes `--write-domain 'task_results/**'` so the subagent can still write its own report).
76	- `read_only: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may only write its `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`.
77	- `can_spawn: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may dispatch children whose `write_domain` is a subset of its own.
78	
79	The dispatcher enforces `write_domain` at the filesystem level: any child whose declared domain is not a subset of the parent's is rejected before the child agent starts. Reads are restricted only by the descriptor's prompt body (e.g. fresh-context critics that are told not to read PROGRESS.md / STRATEGY.md).
80	
81	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`** is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow run by hand by the mathematician. The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. To invoke a refactor-style subagent inside the loop, write the directive to a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md` and dispatch via the generic wrapper. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise.
82	
83	## Protected Declarations
84	
85	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Rules:
86	
87	- **Plan / prover / review** agents: read-only on protected signatures. May fill proof bodies; may not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
88	- **Write-capable structural subagents** (per their `write_domain`): may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (name + signature preserved verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. They may NEVER rename / re-type / delete / re-sign a protected declaration.
89	- The YAML file itself is user-only. No agent adds or removes a declaration. The only allowed agent edit is updating an existing declaration's file path.
90	
91	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
92	
93	Two semantic markers:
94	
95	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
96	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
97	
98	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
99	
100	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves.
101	
102	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
103	
104	## User Interaction
105	
106	The user provides hints in two places:
107	
108	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and resets the file to its bundled template (an HTML-comment format guide + zero bullets) after the plan phase succeeds. The HTML-comment preamble is stripped before injection so a template-only file renders as "no hints" to the planner. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
109	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
110	
111	Archon talks back to the user through **`TO_USER.md`** — a single, *persistent* notice board surfaced as a UI banner. It is **shared and writable by every agent** (plan, prover, review). It is no longer cleared at the start of each review phase; content survives across iters until an agent prunes it. The discipline every writer follows:
112	
113	- **Concise**: the whole file stays ≤ 2–3 short bullets. It is a banner, not a log.
114	- **Relevant now**: before adding anything, read the current content and **delete any bullet that is no longer true at this point** (the blocker was resolved, the decision was overtaken, the credential got set). Never let it accumulate stale notices.
115	- **Notice board, never a question queue**: the loop is autonomous and may run unattended for many iters. Never write "awaiting your decision", an options menu, a "where to reply", or "default to X if no reply" — anything implying the loop is paused for a human. State decisions as *made* and blockers as *worked-around-as-far-as-possible*; the user steers asynchronously via `USER_HINTS.md`.
116	
117	## Agent Roles
118	
119	### Plan Agent
120	
121	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for full instructions.
122	- User hints, blueprint-doctor findings, recent iter sidecars, the subagent catalog, and the references summary are all pre-injected into your invocation prompt. You do not "go read" those files.
123	- Read `task_results/` to collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`.
124	- Optionally invoke subagents (via the auto-injected catalog) before writing prover objectives. Subagent reports are auto-archived to `logs/iter-NNN/` by the Archon CLI.
125	- Write `PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round.
126	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch markers — `\leanok` is owned by the deterministic sync; `\mathlibok` / `% NOTE:` by the review agent.
127	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself.
128	
129	### Prover Agent
130	
131	- Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
132	- Your **active mode** is injected directly into your invocation prompt — read it carefully, it defines your goal, workflow, and constraints for this session.
133	- When your mode specifies `read_blueprint: true`, you are permitted to **read** your assigned blueprint chapter (`blueprint/src/chapters/<your_slug>.tex`) for the informal proof sketch. You may never write to blueprint chapters.
134	- Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
135	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — never edit another agent's file.
136	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your file for file-specific hints.
137	- Do NOT edit blueprint chapters. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker; the review agent applies them.
138	
139	### Subagents
140	
141	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime. The catalog is auto-injected into the plan and review prompts each phase (see "Subagents" above for the wrapper invocation). Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md` / `STRATEGY.md` / phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. Descriptors with `can_spawn: true` may dispatch children (subject to the global `loop.max_parallel` cap).
142	
143	### Review Agent
144	
145	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for full instructions.
146	- Read `proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data.
147	- Write the session journal to `proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (`summary.md`, `milestones.jsonl`, `recommendations.md`).
148	- Update `PROJECT_STATUS.md` (Knowledge Base only — session narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`).
149	- Maintain the semantic blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:` annotations, stale `\notready` removal. Do NOT touch `\leanok`.
150	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify `PROGRESS.md`.
151	
152	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
153	
154	These run automatically between phases:
155	
156	- **Iter sidecar init** — creates `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` at iter start so plan + review have a stable destination for per-iter narrative. Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded.
157	- **`sync_leanok`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status.
158	- **`blueprint-doctor`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically detecting orphan chapters, broken `\ref` / `\uses` / `\proves`, and new `axiom` declarations. Findings are written to `logs/iter-NNN/blueprint-doctor.{md,json}` and inlined into the next plan agent's prompt.
159

6	import Mathlib
7	import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.PresheafCech
8	import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.FreePresheafComplex
9	
10	/-!
11	# Form-B absolute cohomology `H^p(U, F) := Ext^p(jShriekOU U, F)` (P5b input)
12	-/
13	
14	universe u
15	
16	open CategoryTheory Limits CategoryTheory.Abelian
17	
18	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
19	
20	variable {X : Scheme.{u}}
21	
22	/-- The standard `HasExt` structure on `X.Modules`, made a section-local instance so the
23	`Ext`-based absolute cohomology below resolves it without the slow `HasSmallLocalizedHom`
24	typeclass search. -/
25	noncomputable local instance hasExtModules : HasExt.{u + 1, u, u + 1} X.Modules :=
26	  HasExt.standard _
27	
28	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — Form-B absolute cohomology -/
29	
30	noncomputable def jShriekOU (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) : X.Modules :=
31	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).obj
32	    ((PresheafOfModules.free X.ringCatSheaf.obj).obj (yoneda.obj U))
33	
34	/-- The sheafification adjunction hom-bijection as an `AddEquiv` (additivity holds because
35	the right adjoint is an additive functor and composition is bilinear). -/
36	noncomputable def sheafificationHomAddEquiv (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) (F : X.Modules) :
37	    (jShriekOU U ⟶ F) ≃+
38	      ((PresheafOfModules.free X.ringCatSheaf.obj).obj (yoneda.obj U) ⟶
39	        (Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).obj F) :=
40	  { (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).homEquiv
41	      ((PresheafOfModules.free X.ringCatSheaf.obj).obj (yoneda.obj U)) F with
42	    map_add' := by
43	      intro f g
44	      haveI : (SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf ⋙
45	          PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).Additive := inferInstance
46	      simp only [Equiv.toFun_as_coe, Adjunction.homEquiv_unit]
47	      erw [Functor.map_add, Preadditive.comp_add]
48	      rfl }
49	
50	noncomputable def jShriekOU_homEquiv (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) (F : X.Modules) :
51	    (jShriekOU U ⟶ F) ≃+
52	      ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).obj F).presheaf.obj (Opposite.op U) :=
53	  (sheafificationHomAddEquiv U F).trans (freeYonedaHomAddEquiv U _)
54	
55	/-- **Form-B absolute cohomology** `H^p(U, F) := Ext^p_{O_X}(jShriekOU U, F)`. -/
56	noncomputable def absoluteCohomology (p : ℕ) (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
57	    (F : X.Modules) : AddCommGrpCat :=
58	  AddCommGrpCat.of (Ext (jShriekOU U) F p)
59	
60	/-- **`H⁰(U, F) ≅ Γ(U, F)`** as abelian groups: degree-zero `Ext` out of the
61	corepresenting object `jShriekOU U` recovers the sections of `F` over `U`. This is the
62	additive corepresentability of global sections specialised to `p = 0`. -/
63	noncomputable def absoluteCohomologyZeroAddEquiv (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
64	    (F : X.Modules) :
65	    Ext (jShriekOU U) F 0 ≃+
66	      ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).obj F).presheaf.obj (Opposite.op U) :=
67	  (AddEquiv.mk' Ext.homEquiv₀ (by
68	    intro a b
69	    refine Ext.homEquiv₀.symm.injective ?_
70	    simp only [Ext.homEquiv₀_symm_apply, Ext.mk₀_add, Ext.mk₀_homEquiv₀_apply])).trans
71	    (jShriekOU_homEquiv U F)
72	
73	/-- **Injective vanishing**: positive-degree absolute cohomology of an injective sheaf of
74	modules vanishes. Direct from `Ext.eq_zero_of_injective` (the injective object is the
75	*second* `Ext` argument, so no restriction of the coefficient sheaf is needed). -/
76	theorem absoluteCohomology_eq_zero_of_injective (n : ℕ) (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
77	    (I : X.Modules) [Injective I] (e : Ext (jShriekOU U) I (n + 1)) : e = 0 :=
78	  Ext.eq_zero_of_injective e
79	
80	/-- **Covariant `H^p(U,-)` long exact sequence, surjectivity at `H^{n₁}(U, F₁)`.**
81	Thin wrapper around `Ext.covariant_sequence_exact₁` at fixed first argument
82	`jShriekOU U`, for a short exact sequence of `O_X`-modules. -/
83	theorem absoluteCohomology_covariant_exact₁ (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
84	    {S : ShortComplex X.Modules} (hS : S.ShortExact) {n₁ : ℕ}
85	    (x₁ : Ext (jShriekOU U) S.X₁ n₁) (hx₁ : x₁.comp (Ext.mk₀ S.f) (add_zero n₁) = 0)
86	    {n₀ : ℕ} (hn₀ : n₀ + 1 = n₁) :
87	    ∃ x₃ : Ext (jShriekOU U) S.X₃ n₀, x₃.comp hS.extClass hn₀ = x₁ :=
88	  Ext.covariant_sequence_exact₁ (jShriekOU U) hS x₁ hx₁ hn₀
89	
90	/-- **Covariant `H^p(U,-)` long exact sequence, exactness at `H^n(U, F₂)`.**
91	Thin wrapper around `Ext.covariant_sequence_exact₂` at fixed first argument
92	`jShriekOU U`. -/
93	theorem absoluteCohomology_covariant_exact₂ (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
94	    {S : ShortComplex X.Modules} (hS : S.ShortExact) {n : ℕ}
95	    (x₂ : Ext (jShriekOU U) S.X₂ n) (hx₂ : x₂.comp (Ext.mk₀ S.g) (add_zero n) = 0) :
96	    ∃ x₁ : Ext (jShriekOU U) S.X₁ n, x₁.comp (Ext.mk₀ S.f) (add_zero n) = x₂ :=
97	  Ext.covariant_sequence_exact₂ (jShriekOU U) hS x₂ hx₂
98	
99	/-- **Covariant `H^p(U,-)` long exact sequence, exactness at the connecting map.**
100	Thin wrapper around `Ext.covariant_sequence_exact₃` at fixed first argument
101	`jShriekOU U`. -/
102	theorem absoluteCohomology_covariant_exact₃ (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
103	    {S : ShortComplex X.Modules} (hS : S.ShortExact) {n₀ : ℕ}
104	    (x₃ : Ext (jShriekOU U) S.X₃ n₀) {n₁ : ℕ} (hn₁ : n₀ + 1 = n₁)
105	    (hx₃ : x₃.comp hS.extClass hn₁ = 0) :
106	    ∃ x₂ : Ext (jShriekOU U) S.X₂ n₀, x₂.comp (Ext.mk₀ S.g) (add_zero n₀) = x₃ :=
107	  Ext.covariant_sequence_exact₃ (jShriekOU U) hS x₃ hn₁ hx₃
108	
109	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — naturality of `H⁰ ≅ Γ` -/
110	
111	/-- **Naturality of `Ext.homEquiv₀` in the second argument.** The degree-zero `Ext`
112	comparison intertwines composition with the degree-zero Ext class `Ext.mk₀ g` and ordinary
113	post-composition with `g`. Project-local: Mathlib has the comparison `Ext.homEquiv₀` and the
114	composite formula `Ext.mk₀_comp_mk₀`, but not this intertwining as a packaged lemma. -/
115	private lemma homEquiv₀_comp_mk₀ {A B Z : X.Modules} (e : Ext A B 0) (g : B ⟶ Z) :
116	    Ext.homEquiv₀ (e.comp (Ext.mk₀ g) (add_zero 0)) = Ext.homEquiv₀ e ≫ g := by
117	  conv_lhs => rw [← Ext.mk₀_homEquiv₀_apply e, Ext.mk₀_comp_mk₀]
118	  rw [← Ext.homEquiv₀_symm_apply, Equiv.apply_symm_apply]
119	
120	/-- **Naturality of `freeYonedaHomEquiv` in the coefficient presheaf.** Post-composition by a
121	morphism of presheaves of modules `q` corresponds, under the free–Yoneda evaluation bijection,
122	to applying `q` on sections over `V`. Project-local: the underlying bijection is project-local
123	(`freeYonedaHomEquiv`), so is its naturality. -/
124	private lemma freeYonedaHomEquiv_naturality (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
125	    {F₁ F₂ : X.PresheafOfModules}
126	    (ψ : (PresheafOfModules.free X.ringCatSheaf.obj).obj (yoneda.obj V) ⟶ F₁) (q : F₁ ⟶ F₂) :
127	    freeYonedaHomEquiv V F₂ (ψ ≫ q)
128	      = (ConcreteCategory.hom (q.app (Opposite.op V))) (freeYonedaHomEquiv V F₁ ψ) := by
129	  rw [freeYonedaHomEquiv_apply, freeYonedaHomEquiv_apply, PresheafOfModules.comp_app,
130	    ModuleCat.comp_apply]
131	
132	/-- **Naturality of `sheafificationHomAddEquiv` in the coefficient sheaf.** This is the
133	naturality of the sheafification adjunction's hom-bijection in its right (codomain) argument;
134	the right adjoint is `Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X` (definitionally the forget–restrict
135	composite). Project-local because `sheafificationHomAddEquiv` is the project's additive
136	packaging of that bijection. -/
137	private lemma sheafificationHomAddEquiv_naturality (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
138	    {F₁ F₂ : X.Modules} (h : jShriekOU U ⟶ F₁) (g : F₁ ⟶ F₂) :
139	    sheafificationHomAddEquiv U F₂ (h ≫ g)
140	      = sheafificationHomAddEquiv U F₁ h ≫ (Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).map g :=
141	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).homEquiv_naturality_right h g
142	
143	/-- **Naturality of `jShriekOU_homEquiv` in the coefficient sheaf.** Composing a map
144	`jShriekOU U ⟶ F₁` with `g : F₁ ⟶ F₂` corresponds, under corepresentability, to applying `g` on
145	sections over `U`. Project-local: assembled from the naturality of the two project-local halves
146	(`sheafificationHomAddEquiv` and `freeYonedaHomEquiv`). -/
147	private lemma jShriekOU_homEquiv_naturality (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
148	    {F₁ F₂ : X.Modules} (h : jShriekOU U ⟶ F₁) (g : F₁ ⟶ F₂) :
149	    jShriekOU_homEquiv U F₂ (h ≫ g)
150	      = (ConcreteCategory.hom (((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).map g).app (Opposite.op U)))
151	          (jShriekOU_homEquiv U F₁ h) := by
152	  change freeYonedaHomEquiv U _ (sheafificationHomAddEquiv U F₂ (h ≫ g)) = _
153	  rw [sheafificationHomAddEquiv_naturality, freeYonedaHomEquiv_naturality]
154	  rfl
155	
156	/-- **Naturality of `H⁰(U,-) ≅ Γ(U,-)` in the coefficient sheaf**
157	(blueprint `lem:absolute_cohomology_zero_natural`). For `g : F₁ ⟶ F₂` in `X.Modules`, the
158	functorial action of `g` on `Ext⁰(jShriekOU U, -)` (composition with the degree-zero Ext class
159	`Ext.mk₀ g`) corresponds under the `H⁰ ≅ Γ` isomorphism to the sections map `g_U` over `U`.
160	In particular, when `g_U` is surjective so is `H⁰(U, g)` — the transfer used by the
161	surjectivity step of `lem:absolute_cohomology_one_vanishing`. -/
162	theorem absoluteCohomologyZeroAddEquiv_naturality (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
163	    {F₁ F₂ : X.Modules} (g : F₁ ⟶ F₂) (e : Ext (jShriekOU U) F₁ 0) :
164	    absoluteCohomologyZeroAddEquiv U F₂ (e.comp (Ext.mk₀ g) (add_zero 0))
165	      = (ConcreteCategory.hom (((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).map g).app (Opposite.op U)))
166	          (absoluteCohomologyZeroAddEquiv U F₁ e) := by
167	  change jShriekOU_homEquiv U F₂ (Ext.homEquiv₀ (e.comp (Ext.mk₀ g) (add_zero 0))) = _
168	  rw [homEquiv₀_comp_mk₀, jShriekOU_homEquiv_naturality]
169	  rfl
170	
171	end AlgebraicGeometry

6	import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.CechBridge
7	import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.AbsoluteCohomology
8	
9	/-!
10	# Čech-to-cohomology comparison on a basis (Stacks 01EO) — L1/L2 chain
11	
12	Project-local: builds the section-Čech short-exact-sequence (L1) and the
13	quotient-vanishing step (L2) of the 01EO dimension-shift argument.
14	-/
15	
16	universe u
17	
18	open CategoryTheory Limits CategoryTheory.Abelian
19	
20	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
21	
22	variable {X : Scheme.{u}}
23	
24	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — functoriality of the section Čech complex -/
25	
26	/-- The cosimplicial morphism of section Čech objects induced by a morphism `φ` of
27	presheaves of modules, acting coordinatewise by the underlying presheaf morphism on each
28	basic-open section group. Project-local: `sectionCechCosimplicial` has no functoriality in
29	Mathlib. -/
30	noncomputable def sectionCechCosimplicialMap {ι : Type u} (U : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
31	    {F G : X.PresheafOfModules} (φ : F ⟶ G) :
32	    sectionCechCosimplicial U F ⟶ sectionCechCosimplicial U G where
33	  app n := Limits.Pi.map (fun σ : Fin (n.len + 1) → ι =>
34	    ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj).map φ).app (Opposite.op (⨅ k, U (σ k))))
35	  naturality {m n} f := by
36	    apply Limits.Pi.hom_ext
37	    intro σ
38	    simp only [sectionCechCosimplicial, Category.assoc, Limits.Pi.map_π, Limits.Pi.lift_π,
39	      Limits.Pi.map_π_assoc, Limits.Pi.lift_π_assoc]
40	    congr 1
41	    exact ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj).map φ).naturality _
42	
43	/-- The section Čech cosimplicial object as a functor in the coefficient presheaf of modules.
44	Project-local: packages `sectionCechCosimplicialMap` with functoriality so the induced
45	short-complex maps compose and respect zero. -/
46	noncomputable def sectionCechCosimplicialFunctor {ι : Type u}
47	    (U : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens X) : X.PresheafOfModules ⥤ CosimplicialObject Ab.{u} where
48	  obj F := sectionCechCosimplicial U F
49	  map φ := sectionCechCosimplicialMap U φ
50	  map_id F := by
51	    apply NatTrans.ext
52	    funext n
53	    show (Limits.Pi.map fun σ : Fin (n.len + 1) → ι =>
54	      ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj).map (𝟙 F)).app
55	        (Opposite.op (⨅ k, U (σ k)))) = 𝟙 _
56	    rw [show (fun σ : Fin (n.len + 1) → ι =>
57	        ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj).map (𝟙 F)).app
58	          (Opposite.op (⨅ k, U (σ k))))
59	      = (fun σ : Fin (n.len + 1) → ι =>
60	        𝟙 ((PresheafOfModules.presheaf F).obj (Opposite.op (⨅ k, U (σ k))))) from by
61	        funext σ; rw [CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id]; rfl]
62	    exact Limits.Pi.map_id
63	  map_comp φ ψ := by
64	    apply NatTrans.ext
65	    funext n
66	    show (Limits.Pi.map fun σ : Fin (n.len + 1) → ι =>
67	      ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj).map (φ ≫ ψ)).app
68	        (Opposite.op (⨅ k, U (σ k)))) = _
69	    rw [show (fun σ : Fin (n.len + 1) → ι =>
70	        ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj).map (φ ≫ ψ)).app
71	          (Opposite.op (⨅ k, U (σ k))))
72	      = (fun σ : Fin (n.len + 1) → ι =>
73	        ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj).map φ).app (Opposite.op (⨅ k, U (σ k))) ≫
74	          ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj).map ψ).app
75	            (Opposite.op (⨅ k, U (σ k)))) from by funext σ; rw [CategoryTheory.Functor.map_comp]; rfl]
76	    exact (Limits.Pi.map_comp_map _ _).symm
77	
78	/-- The section Čech cochain complex as a functor in the coefficient presheaf of modules.
79	Project-local: functoriality of `sectionCechComplex`, used to form the short exact sequence of
80	Čech complexes. -/
81	noncomputable def sectionCechComplexFunctor {ι : Type u}
82	    (U : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens X) : X.PresheafOfModules ⥤ CochainComplex Ab.{u} ℕ :=
83	  sectionCechCosimplicialFunctor U ⋙ AlgebraicTopology.alternatingCofaceMapComplex Ab.{u}
84	
85	/-- The chain map of section Čech complexes induced by a morphism `φ` of presheaves of
86	modules. Project-local: functoriality of `sectionCechComplex` in the coefficient presheaf. -/
87	noncomputable def sectionCechComplexMap {ι : Type u} (U : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
88	    {F G : X.PresheafOfModules} (φ : F ⟶ G) :
89	    sectionCechComplex U F ⟶ sectionCechComplex U G :=
90	  (AlgebraicTopology.alternatingCofaceMapComplex Ab.{u}).map (sectionCechCosimplicialMap U φ)
91	
92	/-- **{\v C}ech cohomology accessor** `Ȟ^p(𝒰, F)`: the degree-`p` homology of the section
93	Čech complex, packaged as a named `Ab`-object. Project-local thin wrapper so the 01EO chain
94	can refer to `Ȟ^p` uniformly. -/
95	noncomputable def cechCohomology {ι : Type u} (U : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
96	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) (p : ℕ) : Ab.{u} :=
97	  (sectionCechComplex U F).homology p
98	
99	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — product of short exact sequences in `Ab` -/
100	
101	private lemma pi_π_map_apply {J : Type u} {f g : J → Ab.{u}} (φ : ∀ j, f j ⟶ g j)
102	    (x : ToType (∏ᶜ f)) (σ : J) :
103	    Limits.Pi.π g σ (Limits.Pi.map φ x) = φ σ (Limits.Pi.π f σ x) := by
104	  rw [← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, Limits.Pi.map_π, ConcreteCategory.comp_apply]
105	
106	/-- **A product of short exact sequences of abelian groups is short exact.** This is the AB4*
107	content used in the section Čech short-exact-sequence: each Čech term is a product of section
108	groups over basic opens, so degreewise short-exactness of the Čech complexes reduces to this.
109	Project-local: Mathlib has no off-the-shelf "product of short exact sequences" lemma. -/
110	theorem shortExact_piMap {J : Type u} (S : J → ShortComplex Ab.{u}) (h : ∀ j, (S j).ShortExact) :
111	    (ShortComplex.mk (Limits.Pi.map (fun j => (S j).f)) (Limits.Pi.map (fun j => (S j).g))
112	      (by rw [Limits.Pi.map_comp_map]
113	          have hz : (fun j => (S j).f ≫ (S j).g) = (fun j => (0 : (S j).X₁ ⟶ (S j).X₃)) := by
114	            funext j; exact (S j).zero
115	          rw [hz]; ext x; simp)).ShortExact := by
116	  haveI : ∀ j, Mono (S j).f := fun j => (h j).mono_f
117	  -- Epi of the product map: surjectivity, chosen componentwise
118	  have hepi : Epi (Limits.Pi.map (fun j => (S j).g)) := by
119	    rw [AddCommGrpCat.epi_iff_surjective]
120	    intro y
121	    refine ⟨(Concrete.productEquiv (fun j => (S j).X₂)).symm
122	      (fun σ => ((h σ).ab_surjective_g (Limits.Pi.π (fun j => (S j).X₃) σ y)).choose), ?_⟩
123	    refine (Concrete.productEquiv (fun j => (S j).X₃)).injective (funext fun σ => ?_)

119	Key Mathlib hooks:
120	- `PresheafOfModules.freeAdjunction` — the core adjunction.
121	- `yonedaEquiv` — the Yoneda natural bijection.
122	- `PresheafOfModules.evaluation` — sections extraction.
123	
124	─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
125	Step 4 — `cechFreeComplex_quasiIso`
126	─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
127	Type: the augmented complex `K(𝒰)_• → O_𝒰[0]` (augmentation = "sheaf restricted to
128	the cover") is a quasi-isomorphism, i.e., `K(𝒰)_•` is a free resolution of `O_𝒰`.
129	
130	Strategy (Mathlib has no packaged Čech contractibility lemma; build it directly):
131	- Homology of presheaves of modules is computed objectwise
132	  (limits/colimits in `PresheafOfModules R` are objectwise; see `HasColimits`
133	  instance). So it suffices to show that for each open `V : Opens ↥X`,
134	  the complex `K(𝒰)_•(V)` of `R(V)`-modules is contractible.
135	- The sectionwise complex `K(𝒰)_•(V)` is:
136	  degree `p` = `⨁_{I : Fin(p+1)→J} R(V)` when `V ⊆ U_{I(0)} ⊓ … ⊓ U_{I(p)}`, else `0`.
137	- Fix any index `i_fix` such that `V ⊆ 𝒰.U i_fix` (if no such index, the complex is
138	  `0` and trivially exact). The contracting homotopy `h : K_p(V) → K_{p+1}(V)` is:
139	      `h(s)_{i₀,i₁,…,iₚ} = (i₀ = i_fix) · s_{i₁,…,iₚ}`
140	  (extend the multi-index by prepending `i_fix`). Check: `dh + hd = id` at each degree.
141	- Assemble using `Homotopy` / `HomotopyEquiv` from Mathlib (`HomologicalComplex.Homotopy`
142	  in `Mathlib.Algebra.Homology.Homotopy`). From the chain homotopy conclude
143	  `HomotopyEquiv` and hence quasi-isomorphism.
144	- Do NOT route through `ExtraDegeneracy` (it has a different index convention and is
145	  not directly applicable here).
146	
147	Key Mathlib hooks:
148	- `HomologicalComplex.Homotopy` — chain homotopy type.
149	- `HomotopyEquiv.toQuasiIso` (or `Homotopy.toQuasiIso`) — chain homotopy ⟹ quasi-iso.
150	
151	─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
152	Step 5 — `injective_cech_acyclic`
153	─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
154	Type: for an injective `I : X.Modules` and an open cover `𝒰 : X.OpenCover`, the
155	positive-degree Čech cohomology `Ȟ^p(𝒰, I) = 0` for all `p > 0`.
156	
157	Strategy (two independent parts; does NOT need decisions 4–5):
158	
159	Part (a) — Injective sheaf ⟹ injective presheaf:
160	- Apply `CategoryTheory.Injective.injective_of_adjoint` to the adjunction
161	  `PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction α` where `α = 𝟙` for the scheme `X`.
162	  This adjunction is `sheafification α ⊣ (forget R) ⋙ restrictScalars α`, i.e.
163	  sheafification is left adjoint to the inclusion `toPresheafOfModules`.
164	- The left adjoint `sheafification` is exact (it is exact as a colimit of
165	  sheafification-endofunctors over a Grothendieck topology), hence in particular
166	  `PreservesMonomorphisms`. So `Injective.injective_of_adjoint` gives:
167	  `I` injective in `X.Modules` ⟹ `Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules.obj I`
168	  is injective in `X.PresheafOfModules`.
169	- Relevant Mathlib names:
170	  `PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction`,
171	  `CategoryTheory.Injective.injective_of_adjoint`,
172	  `Functor.preservesInjectiveObjects_of_adjunction_of_preservesMonomorphisms`,
173	  `Adjunction.map_injective`.
174	
175	Part (b) — Vanishing of positive Čech cohomology:
176	- By step 4, `K(𝒰)_• → O_𝒰[0]` is an exact augmented complex (quasi-iso from step 4).
177	- Since `I_pshf := toPresheafOfModules.obj I` is injective in `X.PresheafOfModules`
178	  (by part (a)), the functor `Hom_{PMod}(-, I_pshf)` is exact.
179	- Applying `Hom_{PMod}(-, I_pshf)` to the exact augmented complex `K(𝒰)_• → O_𝒰` and
180	  using step 3 (`cechComplex_hom_identification`) to identify the resulting complex
181	  with `Č•(𝒰, I, U)`, conclude that `Č•(𝒰, I, U)` is exact in positive degrees, i.e.
182	  `Ȟ^p(𝒰, I) = 0` for `p > 0`.
183	- This is the Stacks-project proof of `lemma-injective-trivial-cech` (Stacks tag
184	  0BKP and surrounding; see `references/stacks-cohomology.md`).

198	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — PresheafCech (injective transfer) -/
199	
200	variable {X : Scheme.{u}}
201	
202	/-- **An injective sheaf of `O_X`-modules is injective as a presheaf of modules.**
203	
204	This is Part 1 of the {\v C}ech-acyclicity of injectives (blueprint
205	`lem:injective_cech_acyclic`, Stacks `lemma-injective-trivial-cech`): the inclusion
206	`Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X : X.Modules ⥤ X.PresheafOfModules` is the right
207	adjoint of the (exact) sheafification functor, so it carries injective objects to
208	injective objects.
209	
210	Project-local because Mathlib has no packaged "injective sheaf of modules ⟹ injective
211	presheaf of modules" statement; it is obtained here by feeding
212	`PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 _)` (whose left adjoint
213	`sheafification` preserves monomorphisms) to `Injective.injective_of_adjoint`. The
214	right adjoint of that adjunction is *definitionally* `toPresheafOfModules X`
215	(`SheafOfModules.forget _ ⋙ restrictScalars (𝟙 _)`). -/
216	theorem injective_toPresheafOfModules (I : X.Modules) [Injective I] :
217	    Injective ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).obj I) := by
218	  -- the left adjoint of `toPresheafOfModules X` is `sheafification (𝟙 _)`, which is
219	  -- exact and in particular preserves monomorphisms
220	  haveI : (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).PreservesMonomorphisms :=
221	    inferInstance
222	  -- realign the ambient category instance so the `Injective` instance is found cheaply
223	  haveI : Injective (C := SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf) I := ‹Injective I›
224	  exact Injective.injective_of_adjoint
225	    (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)) I
226	
227	/-- **Free–Yoneda representability (per-term bijection).**
228	
229	For an open `V : Opens X` and a presheaf of `O_X`-modules `F`, the morphisms out of the
230	free presheaf of modules on the representable `yoneda V` are exactly the sections of
231	`F` over `V`:
232	```
233	  Hom_{PMod(O_X)}(free(yoneda V), F) ≃ F(V).
234	```
235	This is the free–forgetful adjunction (`PresheafOfModules.freeHomEquiv`) composed with
236	the Yoneda bijection (`yonedaEquiv`). It is the single-multi-index core of the
237	{\v C}ech complex hom-identification (blueprint `lem:cech_complex_hom_identification`,
238	Stacks `lemma-cech-map-into`): taking the product over multi-indices turns this into
239	`Hom(K(𝒰)_p, F) = Č^p(𝒰, F)`.
240	
241	Project-local because Mathlib packages the two halves separately but not their
242	composite as the corepresentability of evaluation by `free(yoneda V)`. The target
243	`(F.presheaf ⋙ forget Ab).obj (op V)` is the underlying set of the section group
244	`F(V)`. -/
245	noncomputable def freeYonedaHomEquiv (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
246	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) :
247	    ((PresheafOfModules.free X.ringCatSheaf.obj).obj (yoneda.obj V) ⟶ F) ≃
248	      (F.presheaf ⋙ forget Ab).obj (Opposite.op V) :=
249	  PresheafOfModules.freeHomEquiv.trans yonedaEquiv
250	
251	/-- **Generator formula for `freeYonedaHomEquiv`.** The bijection
252	`Hom(free(yoneda V), F) ≃ F(V)` is evaluation of the morphism's component at `V` on the
253	canonical free generator `freeMk (𝟙 V)`. Recorded project-locally because this concrete
254	description is what makes the bijection visibly additive (`freeYonedaHomAddEquiv`) and is
255	unavailable as a packaged Mathlib lemma. -/
256	lemma freeYonedaHomEquiv_apply (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) (F : X.PresheafOfModules)
257	    (ψ : (PresheafOfModules.free X.ringCatSheaf.obj).obj (yoneda.obj V) ⟶ F) :
258	    freeYonedaHomEquiv V F ψ
259	      = (ConcreteCategory.hom (ψ.app (Opposite.op V))) (ModuleCat.freeMk (𝟙 V)) := by
260	  simp only [freeYonedaHomEquiv]; rfl
261	
262	/-- **Additive upgrade of `freeYonedaHomEquiv`.** The free–Yoneda hom bijection
263	`Hom_{PMod}(free(yoneda V), F) ≃ F(V)` is an isomorphism of abelian groups: the source is
264	the (preadditive) hom-group of presheaves of modules and the target is the section group
265	`F(V)`. This is the per-multi-index additive core of the {\v C}ech hom-identification
266	(blueprint `lem:cech_complex_hom_identification`); taking products over multi-indices and
267	intertwining with the differential upgrades it to the full complex isomorphism. Additivity
268	holds because, by `freeYonedaHomEquiv_apply`, the bijection is evaluation of the
269	component `ψ.app (op V)` (a module map, hence additive) on a fixed generator, and the
270	component of a sum of morphisms is the sum of components.
271	
272	Project-local because Mathlib packages the underlying bijection (`freeHomEquiv` ∘
273	`yonedaEquiv`) only at the level of types, not as an `AddEquiv`. -/
274	noncomputable def freeYonedaHomAddEquiv (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
275	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) :
276	    ((PresheafOfModules.free X.ringCatSheaf.obj).obj (yoneda.obj V) ⟶ F) ≃+
277	      (F.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op V)) where
278	  toFun := freeYonedaHomEquiv V F
279	  invFun := (freeYonedaHomEquiv V F).symm
280	  left_inv := (freeYonedaHomEquiv V F).left_inv
281	  right_inv := (freeYonedaHomEquiv V F).right_inv
282	  map_add' f g := by
283	    rw [freeYonedaHomEquiv_apply, freeYonedaHomEquiv_apply, freeYonedaHomEquiv_apply,
284	      PresheafOfModules.add_app]
285	    change (ModuleCat.Hom.hom (f.app (Opposite.op V) + g.app (Opposite.op V))) _ = _
286	    rw [ModuleCat.hom_add]; rfl
287	
288	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — section {\v C}ech complex -/
289	
290	/-- **Cosimplicial abelian group underlying the section {\v C}ech complex.**
291	
292	For a family of opens `U : ι → Opens X` (an open covering) and a presheaf of modules `F`,
293	the cosimplicial object in `Ab` whose value in cosimplicial degree `p` is the product
294	`∏_{σ : Fin (p+1) → ι} F(⨅ₖ U (σ k))` of sections over the `(p+1)`-fold intersections. A
295	cosimplicial map `f : [m] ⟶ [n]` acts at multi-index `σ : Fin (n+1) → ι` by projecting to
296	the coordinate `σ ∘ f` and then restricting along `⨅ₖ U (σ k) ≤ ⨅ₗ U (σ (f l))`. This is the
297	covariant ({\v C}ech) variance that the alternating-coface-map complex turns into a cochain
298	complex.
299	
300	Project-local: Mathlib has no {\v C}ech complex of sections of a presheaf of modules. Built
301	as an honest `SimplexCategory ⥤ Ab` functor so that `alternatingCofaceMapComplex` supplies
302	`d² = 0` for free (no hand-rolled alternating-sum identity). -/
303	noncomputable def sectionCechCosimplicial {ι : Type u} (U : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
304	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) : CosimplicialObject Ab.{u} where
305	  obj n := ∏ᶜ (fun σ : Fin (n.len + 1) → ι => F.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (⨅ k, U (σ k))))
306	  map {m n} f := Pi.lift (fun σ : Fin (n.len + 1) → ι =>
307	    Pi.π _ (σ ∘ f.toOrderHom) ≫
308	      F.presheaf.map (homOfLE (le_iInf (fun l => iInf_le _ (f.toOrderHom l)))).op)
309	  map_id n := by
310	    apply Pi.hom_ext
311	    intro σ
312	    simp only [Pi.lift_π, Category.id_comp, SimplexCategory.id_toOrderHom,
313	      OrderHom.id_coe, Function.comp_id]
314	    rw [Subsingleton.elim (homOfLE _).op (𝟙 _)]
315	    simp
316	  map_comp {l m n} f g := by
317	    apply Pi.hom_ext
318	    intro σ
319	    simp only [Pi.lift_π, Category.assoc, SimplexCategory.comp_toOrderHom, OrderHom.comp_coe]
320	    rw [← Category.assoc, Pi.lift_π, Category.assoc, ← Functor.map_comp]
321	    congr 1
322	
323	/-- **Section {\v C}ech cochain complex of a presheaf of modules**
324	(blueprint `def:section_cech_complex`).
325	
326	The cochain complex `Č•(𝒰, F)` of abelian groups with degree-`p` term
327	`∏_{σ : Fin (p+1) → ι} F(⨅ₖ U (σ k))` and the alternating-sum-of-restrictions differential,
328	obtained as the alternating-coface-map complex of `sectionCechCosimplicial`. This is the
329	section complex of the blueprint, kept distinct from the relative pushforward `CechComplex`;
330	it is the right-hand side of the {\v C}ech hom-identification
331	`Hom_{PMod}(K(𝒰)_•, F) ≅ Č•(𝒰, F)`.
332	
333	Project-local: no {\v C}ech complex of sections of a presheaf of modules exists in Mathlib. -/
334	noncomputable def sectionCechComplex {ι : Type u} (U : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
335	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) : CochainComplex Ab.{u} ℕ :=
336	  (AlgebraicTopology.alternatingCofaceMapComplex Ab.{u}).obj (sectionCechCosimplicial U F)
337	
338	end AlgebraicGeometry

102	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — free-presheaf Čech complex
103	
104	This section builds the free-presheaf Čech complex `cechFreePresheafComplex` of a finite
105	open cover `𝒰 : X.OpenCover` as the alternating-face-map chain complex of an explicit
106	simplicial object `cechFreeSimplicial`.  Everything is project-local: Mathlib has the
107	free-presheaf-of-modules functor, the Yoneda embedding and `alternatingFaceMapComplex`,
108	but not their assembly into the Čech free resolution of a cover.
109	
110	We require `[Finite 𝒰.I₀]` so that the index types `Fin (p+1) → 𝒰.I₀` of the degreewise
111	coproducts are finite (`HasCoproductsOfShape`); this matches the finiteness hypothesis of
112	the downstream protected theorem `cech_computes_higherDirectImage`. -/
113	
114	variable {X : Scheme.{u}}
115	
116	/-- **Free presheaf of modules on a representable open.**
117	
118	The composite `Opens X --yoneda--> ((Opens X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type) --free--> X.PresheafOfModules`.
119	For an open `V`, `freeYoneda.obj V` is the extension-by-zero free presheaf of modules
120	`(j_V)_! O_X|_V` of the blueprint: on sections over `W` it is the free `O_X(W)`-module on
121	`Hom(W, V)`, i.e. `O_X(W)` if `W ⊆ V` and `0` otherwise.
122	
123	Project-local because Mathlib provides `PresheafOfModules.free` and `yoneda` separately but
124	not this composite, which is the basic building block of the free Čech complex. -/
125	noncomputable def freeYoneda : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X ⥤ X.PresheafOfModules :=
126	  yoneda ⋙ PresheafOfModules.free X.ringCatSheaf.obj
127	
128	/-- The open underlying the `i`-th member of an open cover, as `(𝒰.f i).opensRange`. -/
129	def coverOpen (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (i : 𝒰.I₀) : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X := (𝒰.f i).opensRange
130	
131	/-- The intersection open `⨅ k, U (σ k)` indexed by a tuple `σ : κ → 𝒰.I₀`.
132	
133	For `κ = Fin (p+1)` this is the `(p+1)`-fold intersection `U_{σ(0)…σ(p)}` indexing the
134	degree-`p` term of the Čech complex. -/
135	def coverInterOpen (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) {κ : Type} (σ : κ → 𝒰.I₀) : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X :=
136	  ⨅ k, coverOpen 𝒰 (σ k)
137	
138	/-- Reindexing along `α : κ' → κ` enlarges the intersection open: precomposing a tuple with
139	`α` keeps a subset of the indices, so the intersection grows. This `≤` supplies the
140	representable index-dropping maps that define the Čech differential. -/
141	lemma coverInterOpen_comp_le (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) {κ κ' : Type} (α : κ' → κ) (σ : κ → 𝒰.I₀) :
142	    coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ ≤ coverInterOpen 𝒰 (σ ∘ α) :=
143	  le_iInf fun k => iInf_le _ (α k)
144	
145	/-- A coproduct injection transported along an equality of indices: `eqToHom` of the induced
146	object equality cancels the index change. A generic categorical helper used to discharge the
147	dependent-index bookkeeping in the simplicial identities of `cechFreeSimplicial`. -/
148	private lemma sigma_ι_eqToHom_transport {C : Type*} [Category C] {β : Type*} (B : β → C)
149	    [HasCoproduct B] {a b : β} (e : a = b) :
150	    eqToHom (congrArg B e) ≫ Limits.Sigma.ι B b = Limits.Sigma.ι B a := by
151	  subst e; simp

153	/-- **The free Čech simplicial object of a finite open cover.**
154	
155	The simplicial object in `X.PresheafOfModules` whose degree-`p` term is the coproduct
156	`∐_{σ : Fin (p+1) → 𝒰.I₀} freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)` and whose simplicial maps
157	reindex the multi-index `σ ↦ σ ∘ α` (along the order map of `α : [p] ⟶ [q]`) together with
158	the representable index-dropping inclusion `coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ ≤ coverInterOpen 𝒰 (σ ∘ α)`.
159	
160	Taking its `alternatingFaceMapComplex` produces `cechFreePresheafComplex` with `d² = 0` for
161	free (the simplicial route avoids hand-rolling the alternating-sum identity).
162	
163	Project-local: this is the simplicial backbone of the free Čech resolution; it has no
164	Mathlib counterpart. -/
165	noncomputable def cechFreeSimplicial (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] :
166	    SimplicialObject X.PresheafOfModules where
167	  obj n := ∐ fun σ : Fin (n.unop.len + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ => freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)
168	  map {n m} α := Limits.Sigma.desc fun σ =>
169	    freeYoneda.map (homOfLE (coverInterOpen_comp_le 𝒰 α.unop.toOrderHom σ)) ≫
170	      Limits.Sigma.ι (fun τ : Fin (m.unop.len + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ => freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 τ))
171	        (σ ∘ α.unop.toOrderHom)
172	  map_id n := by
173	    apply Limits.Sigma.hom_ext; intro σ
174	    simp only [Limits.Sigma.ι_desc, Category.comp_id]
175	    have e : σ ∘ ⇑(SimplexCategory.Hom.toOrderHom (𝟙 n).unop) = σ := by funext i; simp
176	    have eo : coverInterOpen 𝒰 (σ ∘ ⇑(SimplexCategory.Hom.toOrderHom (𝟙 n).unop))
177	        = coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ := by rw [e]
178	    rw [Subsingleton.elim (homOfLE
179	        (coverInterOpen_comp_le 𝒰 (SimplexCategory.Hom.toOrderHom (𝟙 n).unop) σ)) (eqToHom eo.symm),
180	      eqToHom_map]
181	    exact sigma_ι_eqToHom_transport
182	      (fun σ : Fin (n.unop.len + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ => freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)) e
183	  map_comp {n m k} f g := by
184	    apply Limits.Sigma.hom_ext; intro σ
185	    simp only [Category.assoc, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc_assoc]
186	    have e : σ ∘ ⇑(SimplexCategory.Hom.toOrderHom (f ≫ g).unop)
187	        = (σ ∘ ⇑(SimplexCategory.Hom.toOrderHom f.unop))
188	            ∘ ⇑(SimplexCategory.Hom.toOrderHom g.unop) := by
189	      funext i; simp [unop_comp, SimplexCategory.comp_toOrderHom]
190	    rw [← sigma_ι_eqToHom_transport
191	          (fun τ : Fin (k.unop.len + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ => freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 τ)) e,
192	      show (eqToHom (congrArg (fun τ : Fin (k.unop.len + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ =>
193	              freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 τ)) e))
194	          = freeYoneda.map (eqToHom (congrArg (coverInterOpen 𝒰) e)) from (eqToHom_map _ _).symm]
195	    simp only [← Category.assoc, ← Functor.map_comp]
196	    congr 2
197	
198	/-- **The free-presheaf Čech complex of a finite open cover** (`def:cech_free_presheaf_complex`).
199	
200	The chain complex `K(𝒰)_•` in `X.PresheafOfModules` whose degree-`p` term is
201	`∐_{σ : Fin (p+1) → 𝒰.I₀} freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)`
202	`= ⨁_σ (PresheafOfModules.free _).obj (yoneda.obj (⨅ k, U (σ k)))`, with the
203	alternating-sum index-dropping differential. Built as the `alternatingFaceMapComplex` of
204	`cechFreeSimplicial`, so `d² = 0` holds by the simplicial identities.
205	
206	Project-local: the free Čech resolution of a cover is not in Mathlib. -/
207	noncomputable def cechFreePresheafComplex (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] :
208	    ChainComplex X.PresheafOfModules ℕ :=
209	  (AlgebraicTopology.alternatingFaceMapComplex X.PresheafOfModules).obj (cechFreeSimplicial 𝒰)
210	
211	/-- Degreewise unfolding of the free Čech complex: the degree-`p` term is the coproduct of
212	free presheaves over the `(p+1)`-fold multi-indices. Exposed (it holds by `rfl`) so that the
213	downstream hom-identification and quasi-isomorphism arguments can rewrite the degree-`p`
214	object without unfolding `alternatingFaceMapComplex` and `cechFreeSimplicial` by hand. -/
215	lemma cechFreePresheafComplex_X (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] (p : ℕ) :
216	    (cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰).X p
217	      = ∐ fun σ : Fin (p + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ => freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ) :=
218	  rfl

221	
222	/-- **Augmentation of a representable free presheaf onto the structure presheaf.**
223	
224	The canonical map `freeYoneda V ⟶ unit` (= `O_X`) corresponding under the free–Yoneda hom
225	bijection to the unit section `1 ∈ O_X(V)`. Concretely it sends the free generator over a
226	`W ⊆ V` to `1 ∈ O_X(W)`. This is the per-summand component of the Čech augmentation
227	`K(𝒰)_0 ⟶ O_𝒰`.
228	
229	Project-local: Mathlib has neither `freeYoneda` nor its augmentation. -/
230	noncomputable def freeYonedaAug (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X) :
231	    freeYoneda.obj V ⟶ PresheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf.obj :=
232	  (freeYonedaHomEquiv V (PresheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).symm
233	    (1 : (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op V)))
234	
235	/-- Value of the free–Yoneda hom bijection on the augmentation: it is the unit section `1`. -/
236	lemma freeYonedaHomEquiv_freeYonedaAug (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X) :
237	    freeYonedaHomEquiv V (PresheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf.obj) (freeYonedaAug V)
238	      = (1 : (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op V))) := by
239	  simp only [freeYonedaAug, Equiv.apply_symm_apply]
240	
241	/-- Value of the augmentation `freeYoneda V' ⟶ O_X` on a generator `freeMk g` (`g : V ⟶ V'`):
242	it is the unit section `1 ∈ O_X(V)` (the restriction of `1 ∈ O_X(V')`). -/
243	private lemma freeYonedaAug_app_freeMk {V V' : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X} (g : V ⟶ V') :
244	    (ConcreteCategory.hom ((freeYonedaAug V').app (Opposite.op V))) (ModuleCat.freeMk g)
245	      = (1 : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op V)) := by
246	  have key := (freeYonedaHomEquiv_apply V' (PresheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf.obj)
247	      (freeYonedaAug V')).symm.trans (freeYonedaHomEquiv_freeYonedaAug V')
248	  have hmap : (ConcreteCategory.hom ((freeYoneda.obj V').map g.op))
249	      (ModuleCat.freeMk (𝟙 V')) = ModuleCat.freeMk g := by
250	    erw [PresheafOfModules.freeObj_map, ModuleCat.freeDesc_apply]
251	    change ModuleCat.freeMk ((ConcreteCategory.hom ((yoneda.obj V').map g.op)) (𝟙 V'))
252	      = ModuleCat.freeMk g
253	    congr 1
254	  have hnat := PresheafOfModules.naturality_apply (freeYonedaAug V') g.op
255	    (ModuleCat.freeMk (𝟙 V'))
256	  rw [hmap] at hnat
257	  rw [hnat]
258	  change (ConcreteCategory.hom (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map g.op))
259	    ((ConcreteCategory.hom ((freeYonedaAug V').app (Opposite.op V'))) (ModuleCat.freeMk (𝟙 V'))) = 1
260	  exact (congrArg (ConcreteCategory.hom (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map g.op)) key).trans (map_one _)
261	
262	/-- **Naturality of the augmentation.** For `V ≤ V'` the restriction map of free presheaves
263	composed with the augmentation onto `O_X` equals the augmentation over the smaller open:
264	`freeYoneda.map (incl) ≫ freeYonedaAug V' = freeYonedaAug V`. This is the cocone condition
265	that turns the per-summand augmentations into a chain map `K(𝒰)_• ⟶ O_𝒰[0]`. -/
266	lemma freeYoneda_map_comp_aug {V V' : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X} (h : V ≤ V') :
267	    freeYoneda.map (homOfLE h) ≫ freeYonedaAug V' = freeYonedaAug V := by
268	  apply (freeYonedaHomEquiv V (PresheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).injective
269	  rw [freeYonedaHomEquiv_freeYonedaAug, freeYonedaHomEquiv_apply, PresheafOfModules.comp_app]
270	  erw [ModuleCat.comp_apply, Functor.comp_map,
271	    PresheafOfModules.free_map_app, ModuleCat.free_map_apply, freeYonedaAug_app_freeMk]
272	
273	/-- **Degree-`0` augmentation of the free Čech complex.** The map
274	`K(𝒰)_0 = ∐_{σ : Fin 1 → I} freeYoneda(U_σ) ⟶ O_X` assembled from the per-summand
275	augmentations `freeYonedaAug`. Its image presheaf is the cover structure presheaf `O_𝒰`. -/
276	noncomputable def cechFreeAug (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] :
277	    (cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰).X 0 ⟶ PresheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf.obj :=
278	  Limits.Sigma.desc fun σ : Fin (0 + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ => freeYonedaAug (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)
279	
280	/-- **The cover structure presheaf** `O_𝒰` (`def:cover_structure_presheaf`).
281	
282	The image (as a presheaf of `O_X`-modules) of the degree-`0` augmentation `K(𝒰)_0 ⟶ O_X`
283	(`cechFreeAug`). Concretely `O_𝒰(W) = O_X(W)` when `W` is contained in some cover member
284	`U_i`, and `0` otherwise. The free Čech complex `K(𝒰)_•` is a resolution of `O_𝒰`
285	(`cechFreeComplex_quasiIso`).
286	
287	Project-local: the image presheaf of a cover's augmentation has no Mathlib counterpart. -/
288	noncomputable def coverStructurePresheaf (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] :
289	    X.PresheafOfModules :=
290	  Limits.image (cechFreeAug 𝒰)
291	
292	/-- Each face map `δ i : K(𝒰)_1 ⟶ K(𝒰)_0` composed with the augmentation is the same
293	multi-indexed augmentation `∐_{σ : Fin 2 → I} freeYoneda(U_σ) ⟶ O_X`, independent of `i`.
294	This is the cosimplicial-identity input to `d ≫ aug = 0`: the two faces become equal after
295	augmenting, by naturality of `freeYonedaAug`. -/
296	private lemma cechFreeSimplicial_δ_comp_aug (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] (i : Fin 2) :
297	    (cechFreeSimplicial 𝒰).δ i ≫ cechFreeAug 𝒰
298	      = Limits.Sigma.desc (fun σ : Fin 2 → 𝒰.I₀ => freeYonedaAug (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)) := by
299	  apply Limits.Sigma.hom_ext
300	  intro σ
301	  simp only [SimplicialObject.δ, cechFreeSimplicial, cechFreeAug, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc,
302	    Limits.Sigma.ι_desc_assoc, Category.assoc]
303	  exact freeYoneda_map_comp_aug (coverInterOpen_comp_le 𝒰 _ σ)
304	
305	/-- The differential `K(𝒰)_1 ⟶ K(𝒰)_0` composed with the augmentation vanishes: the
306	alternating face sum `δ_0 - δ_1` is killed because both faces agree after augmenting
307	(`cechFreeSimplicial_δ_comp_aug`). This is the cochain-map condition for the augmentation
308	`K(𝒰)_• ⟶ O_𝒰[0]`. -/
309	private lemma cechFree_d_comp_aug (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] :
310	    (cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰).d 1 0 ≫ cechFreeAug 𝒰 = 0 := by
311	  have hd : (cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰).d 1 0
312	      = AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingFaceMapComplex.objD (cechFreeSimplicial 𝒰) 0 :=
313	    AlgebraicTopology.alternatingFaceMapComplex_obj_d (cechFreeSimplicial 𝒰) 0
314	  -- Prove the simplicial-level identity as a standalone `have` so its composition `≫` is
315	  -- elaborated fresh at the `cechFreeSimplicial.obj` types — matching
316	  -- `cechFreeSimplicial_δ_comp_aug`. Rewriting `hd` into the goal first would pin the composition
317	  -- at the `.X` types of the chain complex, which are only definitionally (not syntactically)
318	  -- equal and block the rewrites.
319	  have main : AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingFaceMapComplex.objD (cechFreeSimplicial 𝒰) 0
320	      ≫ cechFreeAug 𝒰 = 0 := by
321	    rw [AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingFaceMapComplex.objD, Fin.sum_univ_two,
322	      Preadditive.add_comp, Preadditive.zsmul_comp, Preadditive.zsmul_comp,
323	      cechFreeSimplicial_δ_comp_aug, cechFreeSimplicial_δ_comp_aug]
324	    simp only [Fin.isValue, Fin.val_zero, Fin.val_one, pow_zero, pow_one, one_zsmul, neg_one_zsmul]
325	    abel
326	  rw [hd]; exact main
327	
328	/-- The differential `K(𝒰)_1 ⟶ K(𝒰)_0` composed with the map onto the image presheaf
329	`O_𝒰 = image(cechFreeAug)` vanishes. Obtained from `cechFree_d_comp_aug` by cancelling the
330	mono `image.ι`. This is the cochain-map condition for the augmentation
331	`K(𝒰)_• ⟶ O_𝒰[0]` (`cechFreeComplexAug`). -/
332	private lemma cechFree_d_comp_factorThruImage (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] :
333	    (cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰).d 1 0 ≫ Limits.factorThruImage (cechFreeAug 𝒰) = 0 := by
334	  rw [← cancel_mono (Limits.image.ι (cechFreeAug 𝒰)), Category.assoc, Limits.image.fac,
335	    Limits.zero_comp]
336	  exact cechFree_d_comp_aug 𝒰
337	
338	/-- **The augmentation chain map** `K(𝒰)_• ⟶ O_𝒰[0]` (`def:cover_structure_presheaf`).
339	
340	The chain map from the free Čech complex to the cover structure presheaf concentrated in degree
341	`0`, whose degree-`0` component is the canonical map `K(𝒰)_0 ⟶ O_𝒰 = image(cechFreeAug)` onto the
342	image presheaf. The chain-map condition `d ≫ aug = 0` is `cechFree_d_comp_factorThruImage`. The
343	quasi-isomorphism claim `cechFreeComplex_quasiIso` asserts this map is a quasi-isomorphism.
344	
345	Project-local: the augmented free Čech resolution of a cover is not in Mathlib. -/
346	noncomputable def cechFreeComplexAug (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] :
347	    cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰 ⟶
348	      (ChainComplex.single₀ X.PresheafOfModules).obj (coverStructurePresheaf 𝒰) :=
349	  ((cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰).toSingle₀Equiv (coverStructurePresheaf 𝒰)).symm
350	    ⟨Limits.factorThruImage (cechFreeAug 𝒰), cechFree_d_comp_factorThruImage 𝒰⟩
351	
352	/-- The degree-`0` component of the augmentation chain map is the canonical map onto the image
353	presheaf `O_𝒰`. -/
354	lemma cechFreeComplexAug_f_zero (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] :
355	    (cechFreeComplexAug 𝒰).f 0 = Limits.factorThruImage (cechFreeAug 𝒰) := by
356	  rw [cechFreeComplexAug, ChainComplex.toSingle₀Equiv_symm_apply_f_zero]
